questions.text: A function f(x) is continuous at x = a if:
questions.explanation: For continuity at x = a: (1) f(a) exists, (2) lim(x→a) f(x) exists, (3) lim(x→a) f(x) = f(a)
Every differentiable function is continuous, but the converse is not true. A continuous function may not be differentiable.

questions.text: The derivative of sin(x) with respect to x is ________.
questions.explanation: d/dx(sin x) = cos x. This is a standard derivative formula.

questions.text: State the chain rule for differentiation.
questions.correctAnswer: If y = f(u) and u = g(x), then dy/dx = dy/du × du/dx
questions.explanation: Chain rule: For composite function f(g(x)), derivative is f'(g(x)) × g'(x)
